metadata.name: Install Red Hat Enterprise Linux by Using Image Mode
metadata.description: Adjust a Kickstart file to use image mode and install RHEL 10 in image mode using Kickstart
objectives: Adjust a Kickstart file to use image mode
Install Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10 in image mode by using Kickstart
Explore the differences between image mode and package mode
prerequisites: Completion of the Create Installable Images for Image Mode exercise

tasks.name: Verify Kickstart uses ostreecontainer directive
tasks.description: The Kickstart file should use ostreecontainer instead of packages section
tasks.success_message: Kickstart file correctly configured with ostreecontainer directive
tasks.failure_message: Kickstart file missing or incorrect ostreecontainer directive
hints: Remove the packages section if present
The ostreecontainer directive specifies the bootable container image

tasks.name: Verify Kickstart has no packages section
tasks.description: Image mode installations should not have a packages section
tasks.success_message: Kickstart file correctly omits packages section
tasks.failure_message: Kickstart file still contains packages section
hints: Delete the %packages section from the Kickstart file
Image mode uses container images instead of package installations

tasks.name: Verify Kickstart file published to web server
tasks.description: The Kickstart file should be available via HTTP on servera
tasks.failure_message: Kickstart file not accessible from web server

tasks.name: Verify serverc is running in image mode
tasks.description: The serverc machine should be booted into the image mode system
tasks.success_message: serverc is running in image mode
tasks.failure_message: serverc is not running in image mode
hints: Complete the PXE boot installation process
After installation, boot from local disk

tasks.name: Verify correct bootc image on serverc
tasks.description: The serverc should be running the webserver-bootc image
tasks.failure_message: serverc not running the expected container image

tasks.name: Verify root filesystem is read-only
tasks.description: Image mode systems have an immutable root filesystem
tasks.failure_message: Root filesystem is not read-only as expected
hints: In image mode, / and /usr are immutable
Try: sudo touch /test.root - should fail with 'Read-only file system'

tasks.name: Verify /etc/motd was updated
tasks.description: The /etc directory should be mutable in image mode
hints: Run: sudo bash -c 'echo "This is image mode." > /etc/motd'
The /etc directory is mutable in image mode

tasks.name: Verify web server accessible from workstation
tasks.description: The httpd service on serverc should serve the expected content

checks.command: curl -s http://serverc.lab.example.com | grep -q 'Hello image mode for RHEL!'

tasks.success_message: Web server on serverc responding correctly
tasks.failure_message: Web server not accessible or incorrect content
hints: The httpd service should be enabled and started in the container image
